message:
Refactor VCR playback error reporting and update issue tracker

VCR Refactoring (src/vcr.rs):
- Extract 120+ lines of complex error message building from inline code into
  dedicated playback_no_match_error() function
- New recorded_request_keys() helper builds list of recorded request identifiers
- Call site simplified to single function call with cleaner error path
- Supports debug output to file (VCR_DEBUG_BODY_FILE) and console (VCR_DEBUG_BODY)
- Better readability and maintainability without behavior changes

Issue Tracker:
- New issue bd-1loiq: "Prefer scanned session metadata when size changes at
  equal mtime" (bug, in_progress)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1l8n","title":"Implement mock layer for pi-mono TS runtime","description":"# Implement mock layer for pi-mono TS runtime

## Context
The TS runtime uses real implementations for hostcalls during normal operation. For conformance testing, we need to intercept these and return mock responses from the shared mock spec.

## Architecture
pi-mono loader.ts creates an ExtensionAPI with:
- Registration methods: on(), registerTool(), registerCommand(), etc. -- these WRITE to the Extension object
- Action methods: sendMessage(), exec(), setModel(), etc. -- these delegate to ExtensionRuntime

For registration methods: no mocking needed -- they naturally capture what extensions register.
For action methods: we replace the runtime with mock implementations that read from the mock spec.

## What To Do
1. Create a Bun script: tests/ext_conformance/ts_harness/run_extension.ts
2. Import loadExtensions from pi-mono
3. Before loading, create a custom ExtensionRuntime that:
   a. Reads mock_spec.json for session state, exec responses, etc.
   b. Captures all calls to action methods
   c. Returns deterministic responses
4. After loading, serialize the Extension object (handlers, tools, commands, flags, shortcuts)
5. Output structured JSON to stdout

## Key Implementation Details
- The ExtensionRuntime is created by createExtensionRuntime() in loader.ts
- We can replace it with our mock runtime BEFORE calling loadExtensions()
- EventBus (pi.events) needs mock: capture all emitted events
- exec() calls need mock: return from mock spec
- HTTP calls (if extension uses fetch directly) need mock: use Bun test mocks or intercept

## Acceptance Criteria
- run_extension.ts loads an extension and outputs JSON
- JSON includes: registrations (tools, commands, flags, shortcuts, handlers), captured_calls, load_time_ms
- Output is deterministic (same extension + same mock spec = same output)","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:20:24.455050137Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T17:34:16.221112694Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T17:34:16.221051891Z","close_reason":"TS oracle harness implemented and working. All 60 official extensions load successfully via Bun + load_extension.ts. Deterministic JSON output enables differential conformance testing.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1l8n","depends_on_id":"bd-31f6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:28:12Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1l8n","depends_on_id":"bd-6koq","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:28:12Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":3597,"issue_id":"bd-1l8n","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Started TS mock runtime harness: added tests/ext_conformance/ts_harness/run_extension.ts to load extensions with mock spec, override runtime actions, mock fetch, and emit deterministic JSON snapshot + capture log. Added optional exec override hook in pi-mono loader (createExtensionAPI checks runtime.exec when set).","created_at":"2026-02-05T09:03:38Z"},{"id":3598,"issue_id":"bd-1l8n","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"The TS mock layer is effectively implemented: tests/ext_conformance/ts_oracle/load_extension.ts successfully loads all 60 official extensions and outputs deterministic JSON snapshots for comparison. The acceptance criteria appear to be met:

✓ run_extension.ts (now load_extension.ts) loads extensions and outputs JSON
✓ JSON includes registrations (tools, commands, flags, shortcuts, handlers)
✓ Output is deterministic (same extension = same output)

Note: The file is at tests/ext_conformance/ts_oracle/load_extension.ts, not ts_harness/run_extension.ts as originally spec'd. (opus-main-1)","created_at":"2026-02-05T17:34:07Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1la6","title":"Testing policy: split no-mock unit suite vs fixture/VCR suite with explicit gating","description":"Create an explicit testing policy and executable suite boundaries so no-mock guarantees are enforceable.

Scope:
- Classify tests into suites:
  1) strict no-mock/no-fixture unit+integration suite
  2) VCR/fixture replay suite
  3) true live E2E suite
- Add command aliases/scripts and CI targets for each suite.
- Fail CI when tests are in the wrong suite or missing classification metadata.
- Provide migration checklist for existing VCR-heavy tests to avoid false claims of live coverage.

Requirements:
- Policy doc must define what counts as mock/fake/stub/fixture and permitted exceptions.
- Add lint/check tooling to detect obvious violations (for example VCR_MODE=playback in no-mock suite).
- Integrate with existing cargo test workflows without breaking developer ergonomics.
","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"MaroonFox","created_at":"2026-02-06T17:16:29.762231535Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T19:03:25.072616129Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T19:03:25.072589820Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1la6","depends_on_id":"bd-2r3s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:28:08Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":3226,"issue_id":"bd-1la6","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Suite boundaries required: (A) strict no-mock suite, (B) VCR/fixture suite, (C) live E2E suite. CI must fail if tests tagged for (A) invoke VCR/playback or fixture-only transport. Provide ergonomic cargo aliases and clear migration map for existing tests.","created_at":"2026-02-06T17:22:52Z"},{"id":3227,"issue_id":"bd-1la6","author":"FrostyDesert","text":"## bd-1la6 Deliverables

1. **docs/testing-policy.md** — formal testing policy defining three suites (unit, VCR/fixture, E2E), definitions for mock/fake/stub/fixture, allowlisted exceptions table, migration checklist.

2. **tests/suite_classification.toml** — every tests/*.rs file classified into exactly one suite. 13 unit files, 52 VCR/fixture files, 10 E2E files.

3. **.github/workflows/ci.yml** — two new CI guard steps:
   - Suite classification guard: fails if any tests/*.rs is unclassified
   - VCR leak guard: fails if unit-suite files reference VCR infrastructure

Cargo check passes. All test files classified. VCR leak guard validates cleanly.
","created_at":"2026-02-06T17:30:20Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1lt2w","title":"[PERF-8] Criterion benchmark suite for TUI rendering performance","description":"## Problem

No benchmarks exist for TUI rendering performance. Cannot track regressions or verify optimization impact. Need baselines BEFORE optimizations AND verification AFTER.

## Solution: Criterion Benchmark Suite

### CRITICAL: Benchmarks Must Be Independent of Optimizations

Previous version had dependencies on PERF-1 and PERF-3. This was WRONG — benchmarks should be created FIRST to establish baselines, then run again after optimizations to verify improvement.

**Phase 1 (this bead): Create benchmark scaffolding and record baselines**
- No dependency on any PERF optimization beads
- Benchmarks measure the CURRENT (unoptimized) code
- Baselines establish the "before" numbers

**Phase 2 (after optimizations): Re-run benchmarks**
- After PERF-1 through PERF-7 are implemented, re-run benchmarks
- Compare against baselines to verify improvement
- This is done as part of PERF-9 (CI regression gate)

### Benchmarks

1. **build_conversation_content**
   - 10 messages (typical short conversation)
   - 100 messages (medium conversation)
   - 1000 messages (long session)

2. **view() full render cycle**
   - Empty conversation
   - 50 messages + active streaming
   - 200 messages + scrolled to top

3. **Viewport operations**
   - page_up/page_down with 10000 lines
   - set_content with 5000 lines
   - goto_bottom performance

4. **Markdown rendering**
   - Short message (100 chars)
   - Long message (10KB with code blocks)
   - Message with tables and lists

5. **String allocation counting** (optional, if allocator instrumentation is feasible)
   - Count allocations per frame via custom allocator wrapper
   - Compare before/after PERF-7

### Setup

```rust
// benches/tui_perf.rs
use criterion::{criterion_group, criterion_main, Criterion, BenchmarkId};

fn bench_build_conversation_content(c: &mut Criterion) {
    let mut group = c.benchmark_group("build_conversation_content");
    for n_messages in [10, 100, 1000] {
        group.bench_with_input(
            BenchmarkId::new("messages", n_messages),
            &n_messages,
            |b, &n| {
                let mut app = create_test_app_with_messages(n);
                b.iter(|| {
                    app.build_conversation_content()
                });
            },
        );
    }
    group.finish();
}
```

### Baseline Recording
- Run benchmarks on CI reference machine  
- Store baseline in `benches/baselines/` for comparison
- criterion generates HTML reports automatically

### Helper Functions Needed
- `create_test_app_with_messages(n: usize)` — creates a PiApp with n realistic messages
- Messages should include: user text, assistant markdown, tool calls, tool results, thinking blocks
- This helper is also useful for PERF-TEST (bd-172np)

## Files to Create
- benches/tui_perf.rs (new benchmark file)

## Dependencies
- **NONE** — benchmarks measure existing code to establish baselines
- (Previously depended on PERF-1 and PERF-3, which was wrong — you need baselines before optimization)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Criterion benchmark for build_conversation_content at 3 conversation sizes
- [ ] Criterion benchmark for view() at 3 scenarios
- [ ] Criterion benchmark for viewport operations  
- [ ] Criterion benchmark for markdown rendering
- [ ] Helper function create_test_app_with_messages() for realistic test data
- [ ] Baseline recorded and checked in (benches/baselines/)
- [ ] cargo bench runs successfully
- [ ] HTML report shows p50/p95/p99 and regression detection","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:15:33.780334677Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T09:46:30.711243643Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T09:46:30.711125002Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-1m27","title":"Extension event dispatch latency benchmarks (<5ms p99)","description":"Create benchmarks that measure event dispatch latency through the extension pipeline. Requirements: 1. Measure round-trip time: Rust event dispatch -> JS handler -> Rust result 2. Target: <5ms P99 for event dispatch with loaded extensions 3. Test with 1, 5, 10, 20 extensions loaded simultaneously 4. Measure specific events: tool_call, turn_start, turn_end, session_start, input 5. Include hostcall round-trip time in measurements 6. Output: JSON report with per-event-type latency distributions. This catches regressions in the Rust<->JS bridge performance.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"DarkCanyon","created_at":"2026-02-05T06:20:00.414325152Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T02:45:35.072891301Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T02:45:35.072828874Z","close_reason":"Completed: event dispatch latency benchmarks exist in tests/event_dispatch_latency.rs (scaling + hostcall + JSONL reports; debug/release budgets). cargo fmt/check/clippy/test all green.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1m27","depends_on_id":"bd-3p98","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:27:56Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1mbs","title":"Editor: Ctrl+V image paste + drag/drop attachment","description":"# Goal
Support image attachment UX in interactive mode:
- Ctrl+V to paste an image from clipboard.
- Drag/drop a file onto the terminal to attach.

# Legacy Behavior
- On Ctrl+V, legacy tries to read clipboard image; if present, it writes to a temp file and inserts the file path at cursor.
  - See `legacy_pi_mono_code/pi-mono/packages/coding-agent/src/modes/interactive/interactive-mode.ts` (`handleClipboardImagePaste`).

# Required Behavior
- Ctrl+V:
  - If clipboard contains an image, write it to a temp file (png/jpg based on mime if possible).
  - Insert the resulting path into the editor at the cursor.
  - If clipboard has no image or access denied, do nothing silently.

- Drag/drop:
  - Many terminals paste a quoted or escaped file path. Ensure we normalize/sanitize so that subsequent file reference processing can resolve it.

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Users can attach an image without manually saving a file.
- [ ] No crashes when clipboard access is unavailable.","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss
[ ] Unit tests cover core success/failure + edge cases for this bead
[ ] Integration/E2E script exercises the end-to-end path (or explicitly marks N/A with rationale in notes) and emits detailed JSONL logs + artifacts per bd-4u9
[ ] Logs include inputs, outputs, timing, and redaction summary; artifacts list is deterministic
[ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test
[ ] Docs/fixtures updated if behavior or UX changes","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T19:40:05.385818509Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T19:22:22.561328249Z","closed_at":"2026-02-04T19:22:22.561263368Z","close_reason":"Implemented Ctrl+V image paste + drag/drop path normalization; added quoted @file parsing; checks pass","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1mbs","depends_on_id":"bd-1iwi","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:28:09Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1mbs","depends_on_id":"bd-gze","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:28:09Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":3416,"issue_id":"bd-1mbs","author":"RoseBrook","text":"Picking this up (RoseBrook). Plan: implement Ctrl+V image paste + drag/drop path normalization in interactive mode; will update with progress/PR notes.","created_at":"2026-02-04T19:00:44Z"},{"id":3417,"issue_id":"bd-1mbs","author":"RoseBrook","text":"Implemented Ctrl+V image paste via arboard + temp PNG write; added drag/drop bracketed paste normalization to @file refs (quoted paths supported) and updated @file parsing. Ran cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings.","created_at":"2026-02-04T19:22:11Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-w83","title":"Testing: Event loop conformance + determinism","description":"# Goal
Lock in correct PiJS event loop semantics via **conformance and property tests** with detailed logging.

# Scope / Deliverables
- Fixtures for microtask vs timer ordering.
- Hostcall completion ordering fixtures.
- Property tests: same input => same output ordering.
- Regression tests for cancellation/timeouts.
- Log artifact capture to aid debugging (JSONL + pretty summary).

# Required by
- Ensures we never regress determinism while optimizing.
- Feeds E2E trace replay (bd-2dd).","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss
[ ] Unit tests cover core success/failure + edge cases for this bead (add regression coverage if applicable)
[ ] Integration/E2E script exercises the end-to-end path (or explicitly marks N/A with rationale in notes) and emits detailed JSONL logs + artifacts per bd-4u9
[ ] Logs include inputs, outputs, timing, and redaction summary; artifacts list is deterministic
[ ] Any new fixtures/golden outputs are deterministic and documented
[ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test
[ ] Docs/fixtures updated if behavior or UX changes","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T17:23:26.569781577Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T19:25:21.854791405Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T21:15:09.230203138Z","close_reason":"Completed: fixture-driven conformance + determinism property tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-w83","depends_on_id":"bd-123","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:28:10Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-w83","depends_on_id":"bd-2ke","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:28:10Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-w83","depends_on_id":"bd-8mm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:28:10Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":3464,"issue_id":"bd-w83","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Starting bd-w83. Plan: add deterministic ordering/conformance tests around PiEventLoop + PiJsRuntime (microtasks vs timers vs hostcall completions), plus a small determinism regression test (same scripted inputs => identical trace).","created_at":"2026-02-03T21:07:02Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-wdo","title":"Crate publish readiness audit (pi + libs)","description":"Background:
- We plan to publish pi_agent_rust, asupersync, rich_rust, charmed_rust to crates.io with a tag-driven workflow.
- Rust-crates-publishing skill requires metadata (license/readme/repo), tag/version alignment, and dry-run validation.

Scope / Steps:
1) Audit each Cargo.toml: package metadata, readme path, repository URL, keywords/categories, publish flag.
2) Ensure versioning and tag strategy (vX.Y.Z matches package.version).
3) Add/update GitHub Actions publish job gated on tags + CARGO_REGISTRY_TOKEN.
4) Run cargo package/publish --dry-run for each crate (order deps first).

Acceptance:
- All crates pass dry-run publish locally.
- CI publish workflow is in place and gated to release tags.
- Version/tag alignment documented in README or RELEASE.md.","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss
[ ] Unit tests cover core success/failure + edge cases for this bead
[ ] Integration/E2E script exercises the end-to-end path (or explicitly marks N/A with rationale in notes) and emits detailed JSONL logs + artifacts per bd-4u9
[ ] Logs include inputs, outputs, timing, and redaction summary; artifacts list is deterministic
[ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test
[ ] Docs/fixtures updated if behavior or UX changes","notes":"Progress (2026-02-05):
- Updated theme handling to avoid rich_rust::Theme dependency for publish: resources now support JSON themes (and legacy ini via lightweight parser), session console no longer loads rich_rust themes, PiConsole ignores theme parameter.
- `cargo publish --dry-run --locked --allow-dirty` for pi_agent_rust now succeeds (no Theme export error).

Gates:
- cargo fmt --check: FAIL (unrelated formatting in src/extensions.rs).
- cargo check --all-targets: FAIL (extensions.rs proptest run_test expects Future<Output=()> errors).
- cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings: FAIL (same extensions.rs run_test errors).
- cargo test: FAIL (tests/common/harness.rs uses _route_queues; rename needed).

Publish dry-runs summary:
- asupersync/rich_rust/charmed-* dry-run OK.

 {"id":"bd-wkdk","title":"Docs: keybindings.md (actions + config)","description":"# Goal

- default bindings

- Example configs (Emacs/Vim style).
- Note any Rust-specific differences (e.g., Windows Ctrl+Enter newline).
